title: Mental Health Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) — UHC Medical Policy
sourceText: Coverage criteria for adult mental health Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP), HCPCS S9480, billed per diem with revenue code 0905. Initial authorization period is typically 4 weeks (12 sessions).

criteria.text: Active DSM-5 diagnosis of a primary psychiatric disorder (e.g. major depressive disorder, generalized anxiety disorder, bipolar disorder, PTSD) documented by a licensed psychiatric provider
criteria.evidenceHint: Look for an explicit DSM-5 diagnosis with ICD-10 code (F-codes) in a recent psychiatric evaluation or progress note, signed by a psychiatrist, psychiatric NP, or licensed psychologist.
criteria.uploadHint: Upload the most recent psychiatric evaluation note from a licensed mental health provider documenting the DSM-5 diagnosis.

criteria.text: Inadequate response, lack of clinical progress, or clinical deterioration at a lower level of care (routine outpatient therapy and/or psychiatric medication management), OR step-down from inpatient hospitalization, partial hospitalization, or crisis stabilization
criteria.evidenceHint: Look for explicit statements documenting why outpatient care is insufficient — e.g. failed outpatient trial with named therapy/medication, recent ED visit for psychiatric crisis, recent inpatient or PHP stay, or crisis stabilization step-down.
criteria.uploadHint: Upload an ED discharge summary or inpatient/PHP discharge summary, or a treating-clinician note explaining why routine outpatient care is insufficient.

criteria.text: Documented severity of illness justifying IOP level of care, supported by a standardized assessment (e.g. PHQ-9 ≥ 15, GAD-7 ≥ 15, LOCUS Level 4, or equivalent) and/or recent suicidal/homicidal ideation requiring close clinical management
criteria.evidenceHint: Look for scored standardized assessments (PHQ-9, GAD-7, LOCUS, CSSRS) with quantitative results in the moderate-severe / severe range, or explicit clinician documentation of active SI/HI requiring intensive monitoring.
criteria.uploadHint: Upload a clinical assessments packet that includes the PHQ-9, GAD-7, LOCUS, and a current safety plan or risk assessment.

criteria.text: Member is medically stable and able to participate in a structured outpatient program; does not require 24-hour psychiatric monitoring (i.e. inpatient or PHP)
criteria.evidenceHint: Look for explicit clinician determination that the member is appropriate for IOP rather than inpatient/PHP — typically a sentence in the psychiatric evaluation or treatment plan stating the member is medically stable and can be safely managed in an outpatient structured setting.

criteria.text: Individualized treatment plan signed by a licensed psychiatric provider that specifies measurable treatment goals, modalities (group therapy, individual therapy, medication management, family/skills training as applicable), program frequency (typically 9-12 hours per week across 3+ days), and the expected duration of the initial authorization (typically 4 weeks / 12 sessions)
criteria.evidenceHint: Look for a written treatment plan document with: measurable goals, listed modalities, schedule (days/week × hours), and an explicit initial authorization duration.
criteria.uploadHint: Upload the proposed IOP treatment plan signed by the program psychiatrist.
